title: Fried Halloumi Salad With Fennel, Quinoa & Roasted Garlic Dressing
summary: We love halloumi. Salty, squeaky, and great in a salad. We've thrown in some quinoa and fennel for a mega dinner.

method.description: Preheat your oven to 200°C. Drizzle the garlic bulbs with a little oil and wrap tightly in foil. Roast for 30-35 mins.

Meanwhile, cook the quinoa according to package instructions. Finely slice the fennel (use a mandoline if you have one), roughly chop the parsley, add the yoghurt, zest and juice of two lemons to a large bowl along with the squeezed out roasted garlic bulbs. Whisk well and season with salt and pepper. Add the cooked quinoa, fennel and parsley before giving it a good stir.

Onto the halloumi. Slice the halloumi into pieces. Heat oil in a large frying pan over a medium heat and add the halloumi, cooking for 4-5 min on each side, until golden. In the meantime, cut the last lemon into wedges. Remove from the pan then serve up a big portion of fennel salad, topping with the halloumi and some lemon wedges!

chefs.name: Jodie Nixon
chefs.summary: Jodie is a recipe writer, Mob food producer, and professional snacker. When she's not recreating all your childhood classics, she's creating delicious veg-forward dishes that'll prove you don't always need meat to have a good time. She's one hell of a talented cook.
